name: Eppo Feature Flags & Experimentation API
what_it_does: Feature flagging and experimentation platform with REST API built for data warehouse-native analysis — storing experiment results in your own data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, Redshift), providing statistically rigorous A/B testing with CUPED and sequential testing.
use_cases: Running statistically rigorous A/B experiments with results in your data warehouse
Feature flag management for gradual rollouts with targeting rules via API
Automated experiment analysis using your existing data warehouse as source of truth
Building experimentation workflows where analysts can design and analyze tests via API
Sequential testing for faster experiment decisions without data warehouse migration
not_for: Teams without data warehouse infrastructure (Eppo requires Snowflake/BigQuery/etc.)
Simple feature toggles without experimentation analysis needs (use LaunchDarkly)
Real-time personalization requiring sub-millisecond flag evaluation
best_when: A data-sophisticated team needs rigorous A/B experimentation with results stored in their existing data warehouse, without moving data to a third-party analytics silo.
avoid_when: You don't have a data warehouse, need simple feature flags without statistics, or want all-in-one analytics without bringing your own warehouse.

auth.notes: SDK key from Eppo dashboard. Different keys for client-side and server-side SDKs. REST API uses separate API key. Project-scoped access. Keys rotatable from dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No public free tier; demo available
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing targeting data-mature organizations. Strong value for teams with existing data warehouse wanting rigorous experimentation. YC W21. Backed by a16z. Growing customer base including Figma, DraftKings.

performance.rate_limits: Flag evaluation is local (no network call); API has standard rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK-based flag evaluation requires configuration file download — not per-call network requests. REST API for flag management and experiment configuration. Strong documentation for data warehouse setup. SDK evaluation is local for low latency.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Flag evaluation is deterministic and idempotent per user and flag configuration. Experiment assignment is idempotent — same user always gets same variant. API configuration operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Data warehouse required — significant infrastructure prerequisite before using Eppo
Experiment results computed in your DWH — ETL pipeline setup required for analysis
SDK downloads configuration periodically — there's a propagation delay for flag changes
Statistical analysis happens in DWH — not real-time dashboard like Optimizely

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth with client/server key separation. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. Experiment data stays in customer's own data warehouse — strong data sovereignty.
